title: Add time-based evolution
overview: Introduce a seeded evolution module that smoothly drifts visual parameters over elapsed session time, making each visit feel fleeting and unique. The module uses the existing PRNG infrastructure to generate per-session modulation curves so that temporal behavior differs between sessions while remaining deterministic within one.

create.purpose: Pure evolution function that applies seeded time-based modulation to VisualParams. Uses createPRNG to generate per-session curve parameters (amplitudes, frequencies, phase offsets) for each evolved property.
Unit tests for evolution module: determinism, smoothness, range bounds, seed variance, no hard resets, at least two params changed.

modify.changes: Track elapsed time from first frame. Pass elapsed to evolveParams after computing base params. Feed evolved params to geometry system.
Add optional 'elapsed' field to FrameState so geometry systems can access elapsed time if needed.

details: Import createPRNG from ./prng and VisualParams from ./mappings
Export function evolveParams(base: VisualParams, elapsedMs: number, seed: string): VisualParams
Use createPRNG(seed + ':evolution') to generate per-session curve parameters
Generate for each evolved param: amplitude (how much it drifts), frequency (how fast), phaseOffset (where the curve starts)
Apply smooth sinusoidal modulation: value = base + amplitude * sin(elapsedMs * frequency + phaseOffset)
Evolve at least: paletteHue (slow drift ±15-30 degrees over minutes) and cadence (±0.1-0.2 oscillation over tens of seconds)
Optionally also modulate paletteSaturation with a slower tertiary curve
Clamp all outputs to valid ranges (hue wraps 0-360, others clamp 0-1)
Ensure at elapsedMs=0 the function returns values very close to base (no discontinuity at start)
Use layered sine waves (2-3 harmonics per param) for organic non-repeating feel within reasonable timeframes
Cache PRNG-derived curve params per seed to avoid recomputing every frame

steps.action: Add elapsed to FrameState in types.ts
details: Add 'elapsed: number' field to FrameState interface (milliseconds since first frame)
This allows geometry systems to optionally use elapsed time for their own evolution
Add import for evolveParams from ./evolution
Track startTime: capture time from first frame callback
Compute elapsed = time - startTime each frame
After computing base params via mapSignalsToVisuals, apply: params = evolveParams(params, elapsed, d.seed)
Pass elapsed in the FrameState object sent to geometry.draw()
For the default-params fallback branch (no seed), skip evolution to avoid errors
Test: evolveParams returns valid VisualParams with all fields in range
Test: at elapsedMs=0, output is close to base params (within small epsilon)
Test: at elapsedMs=30000 (30s), at least two params differ from base by a noticeable amount
Test: determinism — same seed + same elapsed + same base = same output
Test: seed variance — different seeds produce different evolution curves at same elapsed time
Test: smoothness — consecutive millisecond samples have small deltas (no jumps)
Test: no hard reset — scanning elapsed from 0 to 120000 in steps, max frame-to-frame delta is bounded
Test: hue wraps correctly (stays 0-360), other params stay in 0-1 range
Test: reduced-motion amplitude is preserved (evolution does not override motionAmplitude when it's 0.2)
Verify elapsed is passed in FrameState to geometry.draw()
Verify evolveParams is called when seed is available
Verify evolution is skipped in the default-params fallback branch
Run npm run build — must pass
Run vitest — all tests pass
Visual check: scene visibly drifts over 30-60 seconds of observation
Visual check: no jarring resets or discontinuities
Visual check: two different browser sessions show different drift patterns

evolvedParameters.strategy: Slow sinusoidal drift with 2 layered harmonics. Primary period ~90-180s, secondary ~30-60s. Amplitude ±15-30 degrees (seeded). Wraps modulo 360.
evolvedParameters.rationale: Colour shift is the most perceptible long-term change; creates the 'fleeting' quality.

evolvedParameters.strategy: Medium oscillation with period ~20-50s. Amplitude ±0.1-0.2 (seeded). Clamped to 0.3-1.0.
evolvedParameters.rationale: Rhythm change is felt subconsciously; makes the scene breathe differently over time.

evolvedParameters.strategy: Very slow drift, period ~120-300s. Amplitude ±0.05-0.15 (seeded). Clamped to 0.15-0.9.
evolvedParameters.rationale: Subtle saturation shifts add depth without being jarring.

evolutionDesign.curveGeneration: Use createPRNG(seed + ':evo') to draw amplitude, frequency, and phase for each parameter. Each param gets 2-3 summed sine terms with incommensurate frequencies for non-repeating organic motion.
evolutionDesign.caching: Memoize curve parameters by seed string so PRNG is only called once per session, not per frame.
evolutionDesign.reducedMotion: evolveParams must not alter motionAmplitude — that stays governed by the user's accessibility preference.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Elapsed time changes at least two visual: paletteHue and cadence both evolve based on elapsed milliseconds
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Scene does not hard reset during runtime: Sinusoidal curves are continuous; no discontinuities or resets in the evolution function
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Temporal behavior differs between sessio: PRNG seeded with session seed generates unique curve parameters per session
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Motion remains smooth: Layered sine waves with bounded amplitudes and frequencies ensure smooth frame-to-frame transitions; smoothness test enforces max delta

risks.risk: Evolution amplitudes too large causing visual jarring
risks.mitigation: Conservative amplitude ranges; smoothness tests enforce max frame-to-frame delta

risks.risk: PRNG-derived frequencies could produce near-zero or extreme values
risks.mitigation: Clamp frequency and amplitude ranges after PRNG draw
